annotations.label: Extreme value theorem (Euclidean)

anchor.snippet: every continuous real-valued function on a compact space has these properties

annotations.note: The extreme value theorem is formalized as `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn`/`IsCompact.exists_isMinOn` for continuous real-valued functions on compact sets.

annotations.label: Compact topological space (open cover)

anchor.snippet: a topological space is compact if every open cover has a finite subcover

annotations.note: `CompactSpace` is the typeclass for compact topological spaces, and `isCompact_iff_finite_subcover` gives the open cover characterization.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem (lead)

anchor.snippet: For subsets of Euclidean space, compactness is equivalent to being closed and bounded

annotations.note: Stated as the Heine–Borel theorem in proper spaces, which covers Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Compact set (terminology)

anchor.snippet: The term compact set may refer either to a compact topological space

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsCompact` predicate denotes compact subsets and corresponds to the terminology.

annotations.label: Bolzano's theorem on limit points

anchor.snippet: any bounded sequence of points (in the line or plane, for instance) has a subsequence that must eventually get arbitrarily close to some other point

annotations.note: Bolzano–Weierstrass for bounded sequences is in `Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Sequences.lean` (and `IsSeqCompact` formulations exist).

annotations.label: Arzelà–Ascoli theorem (historical)

anchor.snippet: it was possible to extract a uniformly convergent sequence of functions from a suitable family of functions

annotations.note: Arzelà–Ascoli is formalized in `Mathlib/Topology/ContinuousMap/Bounded/ArzelaAscoli.lean` and in `ArzelaAscoli.*` lemmas in `Mathlib/Topology/UniformSpace/Ascoli.lean`.

annotations.label: Heine's uniform continuity theorem

anchor.snippet: Eduard Heine showed that a continuous function defined on a closed and bounded interval was in fact uniformly continuous

annotations.note: Heine–Cantor (continuous on compact ⇒ uniformly continuous) is formalized in `Mathlib/Topology/UniformSpace/HeineCantor.lean`.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel lemma

anchor.snippet: from any countable cover of the interval by smaller open intervals, it was possible to select a finite number of these that also covered it

annotations.note: Same Heine–Borel statement; the finite-subcover form follows from `isCompact_Icc` plus `isCompact_iff_finite_subcover`.

annotations.label: Unit interval is compact

anchor.snippet: A nontrivial example of a compact space is the (closed) unit interval

annotations.note: Every `Icc a b` (in particular `[0,1]`) is compact via the `CompactIccSpace` instance.

annotations.label: Half-line [0,∞) not compact

anchor.snippet: It is also crucial that the interval be bounded

annotations.note: Real line is shown non-compact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace`; the specific statement for `[0,∞)` is not a named lemma but is a direct consequence.

annotations.label: Closed disks and circles compact; open disk not

anchor.snippet: closed disks are compact since for any infinite number of points sampled from a disk

annotations.note: Closed balls in proper spaces are compact (`isCompact_closedBall`) and spheres too (`isCompact_sphere`); open balls in normed spaces aren't compact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace`.

annotations.label: Spheres compact; punctured sphere not; lines/planes not

anchor.snippet: Likewise, spheres are compact, but a sphere missing a point is not

annotations.note: Spheres are compact via `isCompact_sphere`; lines/planes are non-compact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace`; a specific named lemma for the punctured sphere is not present.

annotations.label: Compactness in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: A subset of Euclidean space in particular is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded

annotations.note: Heine–Borel characterization in proper spaces (incl. Euclidean space).

annotations.label: Bicompactness / open cover definition

anchor.snippet: the most useful notion of compactness—originally called bicompactness

annotations.note: Mathlib's `CompactSpace` uses the modern open-cover/cluster-point notion (called bicompactness historically).

annotations.label: Compact topological space (formal)

anchor.snippet: a topological space X is called compact if every open cover of X has a finite subcover

annotations.note: `CompactSpace` typeclass with `isCompact_iff_finite_subcover` providing the open cover characterization.

annotations.label: Quasi-compact and compactum

anchor.snippet: use the term quasi-compact for the general notion

annotations.note: Mathlib's `CompactSpace` does not impose Hausdorffness, so it matches Bourbaki's quasi-compact; the term `Compactum` exists as a category (`Mathlib/Topology/Category/Compactum.lean`).

annotations.label: Compact subset

anchor.snippet: A subset K of a topological space X is compact if for every arbitrary collection C of open subsets of X

annotations.note: `IsCompact` is the predicate for compact subsets in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compactness via subspace topology

anchor.snippet: K is compact as a subset of X if and only if the topological space K is compact in the subspace topology

annotations.note: `isCompact_iff_compactSpace` directly equates `IsCompact s` with `CompactSpace s` (subspace topology).

annotations.label: Characterizations of compactness

anchor.snippet: If X is a topological space then the following are equivalent

annotations.note: Various characterizations exist individually (`isCompact_iff_finite_subcover`, `isCompact_iff_finite_subfamily_closed`, `isCompact_iff_ultrafilter_le_nhds`), but no combined TFAE list.

annotations.label: Bourbaki's definition of compact space

anchor.snippet: Bourbaki defines a compact space (quasi-compact space) as a topological space where each filter has a cluster point

annotations.note: Mathlib's `CompactSpace` (via `IsCompact`) is defined using filters having cluster points, matching Bourbaki's definition.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem

anchor.snippet: A is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded

annotations.note: `isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded` in T2 proper spaces.

annotations.label: Equivalent compactness conditions for metric spaces

anchor.snippet: For any metric space ( X , d ) , the following are equivalent

annotations.note: Equivalences exist piecewise (`isCompact_iff_isSeqCompact`, `compactSpace_iff_seqCompactSpace`, totally bounded + complete), but no single TFAE bundle for metric spaces.

annotations.label: Lebesgue's number lemma

anchor.snippet: For every open cover of X , there exists a number δ > 0 such that every subset of X of diameter < δ is contained in some member of the cover

annotations.note: `lebesgue_number_lemma_of_metric` is the metric-space statement, with a uniform-space version `lebesgue_number_lemma` in `Mathlib/Topology/UniformSpace/Compact.lean`.

annotations.label: Compact metric space is second-countable, separable, Lindelöf

anchor.snippet: is second-countable , separable and Lindelöf

annotations.note: Compact metric spaces have instances making them second-countable / separable (e.g., `secondCountable_of_proper`); individual lemmas exist but a single combined statement is not found.

annotations.label: Compact metric subset is closed and bounded

anchor.snippet: X is closed and bounded (as a subset of any metric space whose restricted metric is d )

annotations.note: `IsCompact.isBounded` gives bounded; `IsCompact.isClosed` (in T2) gives closed.

annotations.label: Equivalent compactness conditions for ordered spaces

anchor.snippet: For an ordered space ( X , <) (i.e. a totally ordered set equipped with the order topology), the following are equivalent

annotations.note: `compactSpace_of_completeLinearOrder` shows complete linear order with order topology is compact; the converse and TFAE bundle aren't a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Complete lattice

anchor.snippet: An ordered space satisfying (any one of) these conditions is called a complete lattice

annotations.note: `CompleteLattice` is a standard typeclass in Mathlib's order hierarchy.

annotations.label: Sequential properties of compact ordered spaces

anchor.snippet: the following are equivalent for all ordered spaces

annotations.note: No combined TFAE for sequential characterizations of compact ordered spaces was found.

annotations.label: Evaluation map is a ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: For each p ∈ X , the evaluation map

annotations.note: Evaluation at a point is a ring homomorphism `C(X,R) → R` via `ContinuousMap.evalRingHom` (or `Pi.evalRingHom` analogues).

anchors.snippet: For each p ∈ X , the evaluation map

annotations.label: Pseudocompactness and realcompactness via maximal ideals

anchor.snippet: X is pseudocompact if and only if every maximal ideal M of C( X ) is real

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of pseudocompactness or realcompactness characterization via maximal ideals of `C(X)`.

annotations.label: Non-standard characterization of compactness

anchor.snippet: a topological space X is compact if and only if every point of the natural extension *X is infinitely close to some point of X

annotations.note: No nonstandard/hyperreal characterization of compactness was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperreal definition of compactness

anchor.snippet: A space X is compact if its hyperreal extension *X

annotations.note: No formalization of hyperreal/nonstandard characterization of compactness in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Open interval not compact via hyperreals

anchor.snippet: an open real interval X = (0, 1) is not compact because its hyperreal extension

annotations.note: No hyperreal-based example for the noncompactness of `(0,1)` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed subset of compact space is compact

anchor.snippet: A closed subset of a compact space is compact

annotations.note: `IsClosed.isCompact` (in `CompactSpace`) and `IsCompact.of_isClosed_subset` give this.

annotations.label: Finite union of compact sets is compact

anchor.snippet: The union of finitely many compact sets is compact

annotations.note: `IsCompact.union` gives binary union; the finite union follows by induction.

annotations.label: Continuous image of compact space is compact

anchor.snippet: The image of a compact space under a continuous function is compact

annotations.note: `IsCompact.image` formalizes the continuous image of a compact set.

annotations.label: Intersection of compact subsets of Hausdorff space

anchor.snippet: The intersection of any non-empty collection of compact subsets of a Hausdorff space is compact

annotations.note: Binary intersection `IsCompact.inter` (in T2) is formalized; an arbitrary-collection version isn't a single named lemma but follows from `IsCompact.of_isClosed_subset`.

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem

anchor.snippet: The product of any collection of compact spaces is compact

annotations.note: `isCompact_univ_pi` / `isCompact_pi_infinite` formalize Tychonoff's theorem, with `Pi.compactSpace` for the typeclass version.

annotations.label: Compact iff sequentially compact in metrizable space

anchor.snippet: In a metrizable space , a subset is compact if and only if it is sequentially compact

annotations.note: `isCompact_iff_isSeqCompact` in uniform spaces with countable uniformity (metrizable suffices).

annotations.label: Finite set is compact

anchor.snippet: A finite set endowed with any topology is compact

annotations.note: `Finite.compactSpace` instance and `Set.Finite.isCompact` give the statement.

annotations.label: KC space

anchor.snippet: A space in which every compact subset is closed is called a KC space

annotations.note: No `KCSpace` typeclass or related abstraction found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compact subset of Hausdorff space is closed

anchor.snippet: A compact subset of a Hausdorff space X is closed

annotations.note: `IsCompact.isClosed` in a T2 space gives this directly.

annotations.label: Compact subset of TVS is complete

anchor.snippet: In any topological vector space (TVS), a compact subset is complete

annotations.note: `IsCompact.isComplete` shows any compact set in a uniform space is complete; specializes to TVS.

annotations.label: Separation of disjoint compacts in Hausdorff space

anchor.snippet: If A and B are disjoint compact subsets of a Hausdorff space

annotations.note: `SeparatedNhds.of_isCompact_isCompact` formalizes separation of disjoint compacts in a T2 space.

annotations.label: Continuous bijection from compact to Hausdorff is homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A continuous bijection from a compact space into a Hausdorff space is a homeomorphism

annotations.note: `Continuous.homeoOfEquivCompactToT2` constructs the homeomorphism from a continuous bijection out of a compact space into a T2 space.

annotations.label: Compact Hausdorff is normal and regular

anchor.snippet: A compact Hausdorff space is normal and regular

annotations.note: `NormalSpace.of_compactSpace_r1Space` provides normality (hence regularity) for compact R1 (in particular T2) spaces.

annotations.label: Compact Hausdorff topology extremality

anchor.snippet: no finer topology on X is compact, and no coarser topology on X is Hausdorff

annotations.note: No named lemma expressing the extremality of compact-Hausdorff topology in the topology lattice was found.

annotations.label: Compact metric subset is bounded

anchor.snippet: If a subset of a metric space ( X , d ) is compact, then it is d -bounded

annotations.note: `IsCompact.isBounded` directly states a compact set in a (pseudo)metric space is bounded.

annotations.label: Extreme value theorem

anchor.snippet: a continuous real-valued function on a nonempty compact space is bounded above and attains its supremum

annotations.note: `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` and `IsCompact.bddAbove_image` together give the extreme value theorem.

annotations.label: Pre-image under proper map is compact

anchor.snippet: the pre-image of a compact space under a proper map is compact

annotations.note: `IsProperMap.isCompact_preimage` formalizes preimage of compacts under proper maps.

annotations.label: Alexandroff one-point compactification

anchor.snippet: Every topological space X is an open dense subspace of a compact space having at most one point more than X

annotations.note: `OnePoint X` is the Alexandroff one-point compactification, with the `CompactSpace (OnePoint X)` instance.

annotations.label: Compact subset of reals has max and min

anchor.snippet: A nonempty compact subset of the real numbers has a greatest element and a least element

annotations.note: `IsCompact.exists_isLUB`/`IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` give the existence of greatest/least elements on nonempty compact sets in conditionally complete linear orders.

annotations.label: Compact ordered space iff complete lattice

anchor.snippet: Then X is compact if and only if X is a complete lattice

annotations.note: One direction (`CompleteLinearOrder` ⇒ compact) is formalized as `compactSpace_of_completeLinearOrder`; the converse is not a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Finite topological space is compact

anchor.snippet: Any finite topological space , including the empty set , is compact

annotations.note: `Finite.compactSpace` is the instance.

annotations.label: Cofinite topology is compact

anchor.snippet: Any space carrying the cofinite topology is compact

annotations.note: Mathlib does not seem to have a `CofiniteTopology` instance with `CompactSpace` proved.

annotations.label: Alexandroff compactification examples

anchor.snippet: Any locally compact Hausdorff space can be turned into a compact space by adding a single point

annotations.note: Mathlib's `OnePoint` compactification covers locally-compact Hausdorff spaces with `T4Space (OnePoint X)` instance.

annotations.label: Order topology on bounded totally ordered set compact

anchor.snippet: The right order topology or left order topology on any bounded totally ordered set is compact

annotations.note: Mathlib's instance handles the order topology on complete linear orders; specific left/right-order topologies aren't separately formalized.

annotations.label: Discrete spaces and compactness

anchor.snippet: No discrete space with an infinite number of points is compact

annotations.note: An infinite discrete space is non-compact: combine `DiscreteTopology` with finiteness arguments; lemmas like `Set.Finite.isCompact` and `discreteTopology_iff_*` cover this. Exact name not verified, treating as partial.

annotations.label: Lower limit topology uncountable not compact

anchor.snippet: carrying the lower limit topology , no uncountable set is compact

annotations.note: The Sorgenfrey-line / lower-limit topology noncompactness statement is not present as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Cocountable topology infinite not compact

anchor.snippet: In the cocountable topology on an uncountable set, no infinite set is compact

annotations.note: No `CocountableTopology` formalization of this statement was found.

annotations.label: [0,1] compact, (0,1) not, rationals in [0,1] not

anchor.snippet: The closed unit interval [0, 1] is compact

annotations.note: `isCompact_Icc` covers `[0,1]`; the open interval and rationals subset are noncompact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace` and basic arguments.

annotations.label: Real line not compact

anchor.snippet: of all real numbers is not compact as there is a cover of open intervals that does not have a finite subcover

annotations.note: Mathlib has a `NoncompactSpace ℝ` instance.

annotations.label: Extended real line is compact

anchor.snippet: the extended real number line carrying the analogous topology is compact

annotations.note: `EReal` is a `CompleteLinearOrder` with order topology, giving `CompactSpace EReal` via `compactSpace_of_completeLinearOrder`.

annotations.label: n-sphere and closed unit ball compactness

anchor.snippet: For every natural number n , the n -sphere is compact

annotations.note: `isCompact_sphere` and `isCompact_closedBall` give compactness of spheres and closed balls in proper spaces (in particular Euclidean).

annotations.label: Alaoglu's theorem

anchor.snippet: the closed unit ball of the dual of a normed space is compact for the weak-* topology

annotations.note: `WeakDual.isCompact_closedBall` is the Banach–Alaoglu theorem (also `WeakDual.isCompact_polar`).

annotations.label: Cantor set is compact

anchor.snippet: The Cantor set is compact

annotations.note: `isCompact_cantorSet` explicitly proves the Cantor set is compact.

annotations.label: Pointwise convergence topology on functions

anchor.snippet: Consider the set K of all functions f : R → [0, 1]

annotations.note: The space `ℝ → [0,1]` is `Pi.compactSpace` (an invocation of Tychonoff); not stated as a named example.

annotations.label: Arzelà–Ascoli theorem

anchor.snippet: A subset of the Banach space of real-valued continuous functions on a compact Hausdorff space is relatively compact if and only if it is equicontinuous and pointwise bounded

annotations.note: Arzelà–Ascoli theorem stated for bounded continuous functions; also `ArzelaAscoli.*` lemmas in `Mathlib/Topology/UniformSpace/Ascoli.lean`.

annotations.label: Lipschitz functions compact under uniform distance

anchor.snippet: Consider the set K of all functions f : [0, 1] → [0, 1] satisfying the Lipschitz condition

annotations.note: No named statement for compactness of the Lipschitz function space `[0,1] → [0,1]` was found; would follow from Arzelà–Ascoli.

annotations.label: Spectrum of bounded linear operator is compact

anchor.snippet: The spectrum of any bounded linear operator on a Banach space is a nonempty compact subset of the complex numbers

annotations.note: `spectrum.isCompact` states the spectrum is compact in a Banach algebra.

annotations.label: Space of Borel probability measures compact

anchor.snippet: The space of Borel probability measures on a compact Hausdorff space is compact for the vague topology

annotations.note: No single instance stating compactness of probability measures on a compact space under the vague topology was found, though tightness/Prokhorov-related results exist.

annotations.label: Prokhorov's theorem

anchor.snippet: Prokhorov's theorem then asserts that a collection of probability measures is relatively compact for the vague topology if and only if it is tight

annotations.note: Multiple versions of Prokhorov's theorem are formalized in `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean`.

annotations.label: Tight collection of probability measures

anchor.snippet: A collection of probability measures on the Borel sets of Euclidean space is called tight

annotations.note: `IsTightMeasureSet` formalizes tightness, with `isTightMeasureSet_iff_exists_isCompact_measure_compl_le`.

annotations.label: Compact real form of semisimple Lie group

anchor.snippet: Every semisimple Lie group has a compact real form

annotations.note: No formalization of compact real forms of semisimple Lie groups was found.

annotations.label: p-adic integers homeomorphic to Cantor set

anchor.snippet: the p -adic integers are homeomorphic to the Cantor set

annotations.note: `PadicInt.compactSpace` shows `ℤ_p` is compact, but the homeomorphism to the Cantor set is not formalized.

annotations.label: Global field quotient by adele ring compact

anchor.snippet: Any global field K is a discrete additive subgroup of its adele ring

annotations.note: The compactness of K\𝔸_K for global fields is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spectrum of commutative ring compact

anchor.snippet: The spectrum of any commutative ring with the Zariski topology

annotations.note: `PrimeSpectrum.compactSpace` is the `CompactSpace` instance for `Spec R`.

annotations.label: Spectrum of Boolean algebra is compact

anchor.snippet: The spectrum of a Boolean algebra is compact

annotations.note: No direct formalization of the Stone spectrum of a Boolean algebra being compact was found.

annotations.label: Structure space of Banach algebra compact

anchor.snippet: The structure space of a commutative unital Banach algebra is a compact Hausdorff space

annotations.note: `CompactSpace (characterSpace 𝕜 A)` instance for proper `𝕜` is in `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Algebra/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Hilbert cube is compact

anchor.snippet: The Hilbert cube is compact

annotations.note: The Hilbert cube `ℕ → [0,1]` is compact via `Pi.compactSpace`; no dedicated `HilbertCube` definition remains (the `HilbertCubeEmbedding` file is deprecated).

annotations.label: Profinite group is compact

anchor.snippet: A profinite group (e.g. Galois group ) is compact

annotations.note: `ProfiniteGrp` builds in `CompactSpace` (via inheritance from `Profinite`); Galois groups are profinite via `InfiniteGalois.ProfiniteGalGrp`.
